title: diervilla floribunda var. versicolor
intro: Diervilla floribunda var. versicolor, also known as the Versicolored or Yellow Bells, is a deciduous shrub native to the mountainous regions of eastern Asia. It is prized for its clusters of small, bell-shaped flowers that change color from yellow to orange to red as they mature. The plant has been traditionally used in herbal medicine for its anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial properties, which can help to alleviate symptoms of respiratory issues, such as bronchitis and coughs. In herbal preparations, the plant is often used to make teas, infusions, and tinctures that can be used to promote respiratory health and relieve congestion.
